listeners.purpose: Fires when a new Tenant is created. Creates the initial TenantMember row linking the tenant's owner User to the tenant with the `owner` role + is_default=true. Skips when the caller already provided a default membership (idempotent).

Fires when a Tenant is being erased. Iterates every TenantMember row on that tenant: if is_default=true AND the User has other memberships, promote the oldest sibling to default BEFORE the cascade delete removes this row (so the User doesn't end up defaultless mid-cascade). Fires UserRemovedFromTenant per row.

Fires when the underlying Identity is being deleted (a real human requested global erasure). Soft-deletes every User row across every Application that references this identity_id, kicking each into its own erasure hold window. Once every User is hard-deleted, `identity::PurgeErasedIdentitiesJob` completes the Identity purge.

Fires when the Identity's email is verified. For every User in status=pending across every Application, transition to active (unless `user.registration.requires_admin_approval` on that tenant is true, in which case leave pending).
Fires on successful login. Updates `users.last_login_at` = now(). Non-queued because it's a single UPDATE per event + we want the fresh timestamp immediately in the /me response.

Fires on every authenticated request. Writes into the `usr:last_active_buffer` Redis SET. UpdateLastActiveJob (every 60s) flushes to `tenant_members.last_active_at`. Non-queued — buffer write is O(1) + must not block the request.
Fires when a Role's permission set changes. Invalidates the `usr:me:{user_id}` cache for every User currently carrying that role (via TenantMember.role_id).

Fires when the sports module (Wave 7) sets `athletes.user_id`. Writes an activity_log entry naming the athlete + fires the /me cache invalidation so the freshly-linked user sees the athlete on their next request.
